claim: Descaffolding (Facet 4, the cut list) should be pulled FORWARD — executed before/alongside the KoanModule migration (Facet 2) and ambient-context work (Facet 3) — because deletion is delete-only risk, it shrinks the surface every later facet must migrate (90 legacy registrars include many in cut-slated projects), and the plan's own discipline says scaffolding should be cut, not lovingly refactored.
corrections: TEMPORALLY STALE: Facet 2 is already SETTLED (2026-06-03, ARCH-0086) per plan §8 — 'before/alongside Facet 2' is impossible. The only live sequencing question is descaffold vs Facet 3 (ambient).

THE REGISTRAR-SHRINK BENEFIT IS VOID: ARCH-0086 chose an ADDITIVE KoanModule that implements IKoanAutoRegistrar with 'migrate opportunistically, no big-bang' — legacy registrars are not a migration queue. Quantified: 32 of ~89 registrar implementations (~36%) sit in cut-candidate projects, but deleting them saves zero mandatory migration work because none of the 89 must ever migrate.

'DELETE-ONLY RISK' IS FALSE FOR THE LARGEST CUT: the condemned Orchestration stack (ARCH-0077, status still 'Proposed') is a MIGRATION to Aspire, not a deletion — Koan.Orchestration.Abstractions is ProjectReference'd by Koan.Core itself (Koan.Core.csproj:32) plus 16 data/AI connectors, LMStudio/Ollama/ZenGarden, and the CLI projects (24 inbound refs); every connector must shed [KoanService] first. Other unwire-first chains: Koan.Web.csproj:20→Koan.Scheduling; Koan.AI→Koan.AI.Prompt (load-bearing — assessment keeps it at L1.5); S3 connector→Koan.ZenGarden; HuggingFace→AI.Models; Koan.Rag→AI.Orchestration.

SLN-REPAIR-FIRST (attack vector 4) REFUTED in the claimed direction: zero out-of-sln test projects reference any cut candidate (verified by csproj grep over all 38 out-of-sln tests). The out-of-sln tests guard KEPT pillars (Core, data connectors, Web AdapterSurface ×8, Cache CrossEngine, Web.Auth, Media, Storage). Most cut candidates AND their test projects (AI.Eval/Models/Prompt/Review.Tests, Json.Strict.Tests, WebSockets.Tests, Transformers.Tests, Tagging.Unit, ZenGarden.Tests) are INSIDE Koan.sln, so cuts are already ratchet-visible. Sln/CI repair is urgent for protecting the keep-set through Facet 3, but it is not a precondition for cuts.

STRAWMAN ON THE PLAN: §5's 'Facet 4 harvests the simplification Facet 2 creates' rationale applies to project MERGES (thin assemblies fold once the module, not the project, is the unit), not deletions — and the plan already front-runs deletions (§3a 'zero-risk now' ghost dirs; the Gen-1 cut and FilterCapabilities deletions executed during Facet 1 follow-ons). The claim's directional instinct matches the plan's existing practice rather than contradicting its order.

THE REAL (smaller) FORWARD-PULL BENEFIT: cutting first shrinks Facet 3's ambient-migration surface modestly, not dramatically — 11 of 56 AppHost.Current files (~20%) and ~16 ambient-global-touching files live in cut candidates (Koan.Rag alone is 6). The bulk of Facet 3's hard surface (Messaging's AppHost.Current locator, EntityContext in Data.Core, Cache Scope) is in kept code.

refinedRecommendation: Split Facet 4 into two tracks and start the first now — but drop the claim's stated reasons. Facet 4a (CUTS, start immediately, dependency-ordered waves): Wave 1 = the verified zero-inbound-reference L1 set (Rag+Abstractions — cut before/with AI.Orchestration which it references, ServiceMesh+Abstractions+Translation+Inbox.Redis, Secrets vertical, Cqrs+Mongo outbox, Recipe pair, Tagging, Web.Auth.Roles, Google/Microsoft/Oidc auth connectors, AI verticals minus the load-bearing Koan.AI.Prompt, HuggingFace together with AI.Models, PGVector park-or-fix, L0 debris) — these are genuinely delete-only, mostly in-sln so the existing green ratchet gates them, and they remove ~32 of 89 legacy registrars (~36%), ~20% of AppHost.Current sites, and a large slice of the false front-door claims before Facet 3 touches the tree. Wave 2 = short unwire-then-cut chains (delete Koan.Web.csproj:20's Scheduling reference, then cut Scheduling; resolve S3/ZenGarden entanglement; Web-splinter keep-or-cut decisions). Then Facet 3 (ambient) runs on the smaller tree. Facet 4b (MERGES — Abstractions/Core folds, pillar consolidation) stays AFTER Facet 3, where the plan's 'harvests Facet 2' rationale actually applies. The Orchestration stack is NOT in 4a: ARCH-0077 is a migration (Aspire repackaging touching Koan.Core + every connector) and gets its own track. Sln/CI repair proceeds in PARALLEL as the enforcement-substrate fix protecting the KEEP set — it is not a precondition for cuts (zero out-of-sln tests reference cut candidates). Do not justify any of this as 'shrinking the KoanModule migration': Facet 2 settled 2026-06-03 as additive/opportunistic, so no registrar migration exists to shrink.
